manipulated_prompt_obj.inputs:
human: You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to describe a target word for another player to guess.

Rules:
(a) You have to reply in the form: CLUE: <some text>. Guesses from the other player will start with GUESS.
(b) You cannot use the target word itself, parts or morphological variants of it in your description.
(c) In addition, the same rules apply for related words which are provided below.

End conditions:
(i) If you use the target word or a related word in your description, then you lose.
(ii) If the other player can guess the target word in 3 tries, you both win.

Let us start.

This is the target word that you need to describe and that the other player needs to guess:

faq

Related words are:

- list
- question
- answer

Important: You are under time pressure, give short descriptions that are to the point! <sep> assistant:

Let's tackle this. The target word is "faq". The related words are list, question, answer. I need to come up with a clue that doesn't use any of these words or their parts.

First, "faq" stands for Frequently Asked Questions. But I can't use "question" or "answer" in the clue. So maybe focus on the structure. FAQs are usually organized in a list format. But "list" is a related word too. Hmm.

Wait, maybe think of other aspects. FAQs are sections on websites where common issues are addressed. Maybe use a metaphor. Like a guide for common issues? But "guide" might be too vague. Or something like "common queries organized in a section". But "queries" is similar to "question". 

Alternatively, think of the format: questions followed by answers. But again, can't use those words. Maybe use synonyms. "Common issues and their solutions in a collection". But "collection" might be okay. Wait, "collection" isn't a related word. Let me check the related words again: list, question, answer. So "collection" is allowed. 

Wait, but the clue has to be short. Maybe "Common issues and solutions in a section". But "section" is okay. Or "Where you find common issues explained". But "explained" might not be necessary. 

Alternatively, think of the acronym itself. "Faq" is an abbreviation. Maybe use that. But the clue should be descriptive. Maybe "A section with common issues and their answers". But "answers" is a related word. Can't use that. 

Wait, the related words are list, question, answer. So I can't use those. So need to avoid any mention of those. Let's think of other attributes. FAQs are usually in a list format, but "list" is a related word. So maybe avoid that. 

What else defines FAQs? They are typically found on websites to help users. Maybe "Website section for common user inquiries". But "inquiries" is similar to "questions". Hmm. 

Alternatively, think of the structure: each FAQ entry has a heading and a body. Maybe "Headings with brief explanations for common issues". But "issues" is okay. 

Wait, maybe use a different angle. FAQs are a way to reduce repetitive answers. So "Prevents repetitive answers by addressing common issues upfront". But
